characters.name: Character Guide
characters.description: This is a complete character guide for theFoster's Home for Imaginary Friendsseries. It includes the main cast, recurring characters, guest villains, and supporting background characters. Craig McCracken, the series creator, designed the entire main cast and most of the recurring characters. Supporting character designs were provided by the series' creative team. Bloo is the main protagonist of the series andMac'simaginary friend, as well as his best friend. He is an anthropomorphic blue domed cylinder who was created by Mac when he was three years old. Bloo is often very self-centered, egotistical, and impulsive, possessing a knack for getting into trouble and breaking the house's rules. His shortsightedness oftentimes prevents him from considering or fully understanding the consequences of his actions. He also has a tendency to blame others for his own shortcomings as opposed to taking responsibility for his actions. Despite all of this, Bloo still retains a good heart and apologizes for his wrongdoings, recognizing when he has gone too far and attempting to make it right in the end.

characters.name: Abominable Snowman
characters.description: Abominable SnowmanCharacter informationSpeciesYeti (possibly imaginary)Professional StatusLike(s)Frolicking in the snowDislike(s)Coco coming home latePersonal StatusFriend(s)Coco(girlfriend)MediaOnly Appearance"Mondo Coco"Voice ActorKevin Michael Richardson TheAbominable Snowman(or simply theYeti) is a character who appears in "Mondo Coco". He becomesCoco'sboyfriend after rescuing her from an avalanche. The Abominable Snowman is a large, muscular ape-like creature with fluffy white fur covering every part of his body except for his fingers and toes, which are large and blue. His design is similar to most other depictions of a yeti, with his only unique feature being a long, prominent mustache.

characters.name: Abraham Lincoln Pen
characters.description: Abraham Lincoln PenCharacter informationSpeciesImaginary FriendGenderMale ♂Professional StatusLike(s)Money, conning people, hot lady pens, deviled eggsDislike(s)Sleeping in closets, being usurped or dethronedPersonal StatusFriend(s)MooseEnemy(s)FrankieMacMr. HerrimanMadame FosterMac's TeacherMediaOnly Appearance"Emancipation Complication"Voice ActorTom Kenny TheAbraham Lincoln Pen(also referred to asLittle Lincolnor simplyAbraham Lincoln) is the main antagonist ofEmancipation Complication. He lives in the pocket of a big, burly imaginary friend namedMoosewho serves as his bodyguard, due to his small size. He is a con artist, his only appearance beingEmancipation Complication. Little Lincoln appeared inEmancipation Complicationas the featured character and the main antagonist of the episode. At first, Little Lincoln seemed like a poor imaginary friend created for the sole purpose of cheating on history tests and unfairly punished byMac's Teacher. But it turned out he was really a con artist who managed to sell a lot of the imaginary friends for a hefty profit, which he would later use to turn Foster's into a casino. The friends were promptly put to work doing anything they were told to do (for example, washing cars for people they were sold to.)  He proceeded to attempt to conMadame Fosterout of the house as well. However, Madame Foster eventually found him out and forced him to return all of the friends to Foster's and refund every person that he had sold them to.

He was named the winner of the annual imaginary friend talent show. Armpit Joe is a anthropomorphic light pink octopus with a red and white cap, yellow eyes, six arms, dark pink dots on his head, and a red tie.

characters.name: Balloty
characters.description: BallotyCharacter informationSpeciesImaginary FriendGenderMale ♂MediaOnly AppearanceSetting a President Ballotyis a minor imaginary friend inFoster's Home for Imaginary Friends. He only appears in the episode, "Setting a President." Balloty is a grey-blue vote-counting machine friend with black arms and legs, a ballot slot on his forehead, white circle and purple checkmark graphics on his chest, and facial appearances on his monitors. Whenever he reveals voting results, the candidate's name covers his eyes, while the number of votes said they received covers his mouth.

characters.description: Barry BlingCharacter informationSpeciesDisguise byBerryGenderMale ♂MediaOnly AppearanceAffair Weather Friends Barry BlingisBerry's alter ego who only appeared in the episode "Affair Weather Friends". Barry Bling is short, has light colored blonde hair, wears a baseball cap with an uppercase "B" on it, a purple suit with a white collar, and white trousers and black leather shoes.

characters.name: Belly Bob Norton
characters.description: Belly Bob NortonCharacter informationSpeciesImaginary FriendGenderMale ♂Professional StatusLike(s)Nuts, opera singingDislike(s)Not having clean underwearPersonal StatusFriend(s)WiltSloppy MoeCocoPirate PigSally LindaLingfromKip and DipEnemy(s)DuchessMayor PeanutBendy(possibly)MediaFirst AppearanceHouse of Bloo'sLast AppearanceGoodbye to BlooVoice ActorTom KennyBelly Bob Nortonis a resident of Foster's and a recurring background character who makes various appearances. Belly Bob Norton is a large, dark green imaginary friend with curly hair with mint-green spots, a mint-green nose, fat, hairy arms and a large, hairy belly. He wears an olive-green checkered shirt with a white shirt collar, olive green pants with rolled up pant sleeves and black shoes. According toFrankie Fosterin aComplete Season 2DVD extra, Belly Bob Norton is a practicing opera singer who can hold a single note for over 40 seconds. This talent, combined with his deep baritone voice, has made him fairly popular with the single older ladies in town. His goal is to become the greatest professional imaginary opera singer in the world. His singing voice is never heard in any episodes, however.

Bendy is a yellow (shown a little bit green) imaginary friend with large, black eyebrows, with a long and spiky hairdo that sticks out horizontally on the back of his head, and has black, zig-zagging stripes from the neck down.

characters.name: Benjamin Edward Factor III
characters.description: Benjamin Edward Factor IIINickname(s)Ben E. FactorMr. FactorCharacter informationSpeciesHumanGenderMale ♂Professional StatusLike(s)GalasCompetitionsCompetitive sportsSarcasmWatching others get hurtFraudDislike(s)Visible imaginary friendsPersonal StatusFriend(s)Mr. Herriman(formerly)Coco(formerly)Enemy(s)MacBlooMediaOnly Appearance"My So Called Wife"Voice ActorPhil LaMarrBenjamin Edward Factor III, Esq. DDS (commonly known asBen E. Factor) is a self-proclaimed benefactor who only appears in "My So Called Wife". Mr. Factor appears to be mentally unstable. He seems to be completely unbothered by any sort of death first-hand (as shown during the jousting segment of the gala when both jousting cars, one containingMr. HerrimanandCocoand the other containingMacandBloo, were about collide, possibly killing everyone involved.) In fact, he seemed to enjoy the idea of witnessing said carnage, as he delightfully states that he has "the best job ever" upon discovering that the two teams were about to crash into each other. Because of this, it is possible for Mr. Factor to be considered a borderline sociopath. With Coco's wackiness even more prominent than ever, Mr. Herriman informs Mac and Bloo that a rich benefactor (being Mr. Ben E. Factor) is considering giving Foster's an enormous sum of money to see if it is a worthy institution. Fearing that Coco could scare off the benefactor, Herriman enlists Mac and Bloo to keep Coco out of the way long enough to persuade the benefactor into providing the money, and in return he will reward them both with jet cars (per Bloo's request). Once Mr. Factor arrives, the task of keeping Coco under control is difficult for both Mac and Bloo, but the duo manages to keep her out of sight for the most part. Unfortunately, Mr. Factor initially disapproves of Foster's for not having "invisible" imaginary friends (apparently unaware that in the Foster's universe, "imaginary" and "invisible" do not mean the same thing, as stated by Herriman) and is ready to leave the premise until he finally discovers Coco and mistakenly believes her to be Mr. Herriman's wife. With his mind changed, he invites Herriman and Coco to attend his "Annual Charity Gala" at his mansion, with the prize being the money he promised to donate to the house. With little time to teach Coco how to be refined and well-mannered before the Gala, Mr. Herriman begins teaching her about how to be sophisticated.

characters.name: Bingo
characters.description: BingoCharacter informationSpeciesImaginary FriendGenderMale ♂Professional StatusLike(s)Kicking trash bagsDislike(s)HelpingFrankiewith groceriesPersonal StatusFriend(s)Fluffer NutterMadame FosterEduardoEnemy(s)FrankieMediaFirst Appearance"Cuckoo for Coco Cards"Last Appearance"Goodbye to Bloo"Voice ActorTom KaneBingois an imaginary friend who appears in a few episodes throughout the series starting in season 3. Bingo is a yellow dog-like imaginary friend who wears a pair of white underwear and a green hat. Bingo made his debut in "Cuckoo for Coco Cards".Bloohas collected almost everyCocoCard in existence, with the exception of one: the Bloo card. When he realizes this,Macyells out, "Bingo!", but Bloo tells him that he already has Bingo's card. Later, Bloo spots a Bloo card in the spokes ofEduardo's bicycle and yells "Bingo!", and Bingo then appears thinking that Bloo was addressing him. Bingo has since made occasional non-speaking cameos.

characters.name: Birthday Cat
characters.description: Birthday Cat is a minor character seen in a few episodes. Birthday Cat is a pink cat with a red party hat. In the episodeWhen There’s a Wilt, There’s a Way, Birthday Cat seems to be seen with 2 other imaginary friends. One imaginary friend asks Wilt. Then, They were eating potato chips.

characters.name: Blake Superior
characters.description: Blake SuperiorCharacter informationSpeciesImaginary FriendGenderMale ♂Professional StatusLike(s)Flying around town with his creatorThe imaginary friend talent showWinningBurpingDislike(s)LosingBeing outperformedPersonal StatusCreator(s)Richie WildebratFriend(s)Richie WildebratEnemy(s)BlooMacArmpit JoeMediaOnly Appearance"Hiccy Burp"Voice ActorPhil LaMarr Blake Superioris a tiger superhero imaginary friend created byRichie Wildabrat. He is featured in the episode "Hiccy Burp". He can fly and is considered by Richie to be the best imaginary friend ever. Richie entered Blake in the imaginary friend talent show pageant where he copiedBloo's hiccup act in burp form; but Bloo managed to get new hiccups after losing his old ones and beat Blake Superior but lost toArmpit Joe.

characters.name: Bloo Clones
characters.description: Several ofBloo'sclones in the foyer TheBloo Clonesare an army of imaginary friends who either look and sound near-identical toBlooor resemble him in some way. Each of them was imagined by a kid atMac's school. WhenMacbrings Bloo into his class for show-and-tell as part of a presentation to promote Foster's, his classmates take an immediately liking to him and decide to create their own versions of Bloo, despite the point of Mac's presentation persuading the kids to adopt an existing imaginary friend rather than create a new one. The next day, Mac's classmates visit Foster's one-by-one to drop off their copy of Bloo for various reasons, the most common of which was their dissatisfaction with their creation not living up to three likeness of the realBloo.

The next day, Mac's classmates visit Foster's one-by-one to drop off their copy of Bloo for various reasons, the most common of which was their dissatisfaction with their creation not living up to three likeness of the realBloo.

characters.name: Bloppy Pants
characters.description: Bloppy PantsNickname(s)Mr. SensitiveHomeFoster's Home For Imaginary Friends(1123 Wilson Way)Character informationSpeciesImaginary Friend(cat)GenderMale ♂Professional StatusOccupation(s)Vocalist forPizza PartyShareholder(briefly in "Read 'Em and Weep")Rodeo clown(briefly; quit after almost getting injured)Student at an unnamed university(dropped out)Line cook at a diner(quit after not understanding the local terms for the food items)Like(s)Kitty treatsSingingHis scratching postBrushing his teethCookingDislike(s)HeightsThe outside worldPersonal StatusCreator(s)Unnamed man in scarf and sweaterFriend(s)Yogi Boo BooBilly the SquidFluffer NutterMacBlooJackie KhonesSam BurgerEnemy(s)Heavy GMediaFirst Appearance"House of Bloo's"Last Appearance"Goodbye to Bloo"Voice ActorJeff Bennett Bloppy Pants(alternately spelledBloppypants) is a secondary character fromFoster's Home for Imaginary Friends. He is a very nervous cat friend who is also the lead singer of the bandPizza Party. Bloppy Pants resembles a grey cat. He usually wears a brownish-orange scarf, a grey shirt with long sleeves, black pants, and black shoes. His whiskers often look curly and unbrushed. On the back of his head are two black stripes.

characters.name: Bob Zombie
characters.description: Bob Zombieis an imaginary friend atFoster's Home for Imaginary Friends. Bob Zombie first appears in the comic "Friend or Foe", where he is shown being carried by Bloo, thinking he is a real zombie who wants to eat his brains. Bob later appears in the episode "Nightmare on Wilson Way". Upon realizing that the whole house pranked Bloo by pretending to be zombies, Bloo asks if nobody really wants to eat his brains. Bob initially plans on saying something but changes his mind at the last minute.

VurmClambakeFluffer NutterPokey ToehairMediaOnly Appearance"Phone Home"Voice ActorKeith Ferguson TheBone Friendis an imaginary friend who appears in the episode "Phone Home". He is rescued byWiltand taken back to Foster's. He is an imaginary friend who has the appearance of a typical white cartoon dog bone with two arms, two legs, and a face.

characters.name: Boomer
characters.description: BoomerCharacter informationSpeciesImaginary FriendGenderMale ♂MediaVoice ActorKeith Ferguson Boomeris an imaginary friend who debuted in season 5. Boomer is a blueish boombox with a handle, antenna, two radio dials as eyes and a cassette box for a mouth, among other typical boombox features. He has thin, black arms and legs and wears purple checkered slip-on shoes and a white glove on his right hand.

characters.name: Boris the Florist
characters.description: Boris the FloristCharacter informationSpeciesImaginary FriendGenderMale ♂Professional StatusLike(s)GardeningMaking bouquetsEating answering machinesDislike(s)Being sent to his roomPersonal StatusCreator(s)Unnamed man in red shirt who was seen handing out free samples inStore WarsFriend(s)Madame FosterSocket TubeySunset JunctionSassyfrassYogi Boo BooEnemy(s)Mr. HerrimanBendy(possibly)MediaFirst AppearanceHouse of Bloo'sLast AppearanceGoodbye to Bloo Boris the Floristis a background character that appears in various episodes ofFoster's Home for Imaginary Friends. Boris is a tall, large, furry imaginary friend who seems to represent a mix of a boar and ape. His oval-shaped body is pale pink at the front and dark pink elsewhere. He has black hair, a thick unibrow, stubble on his chin, a dark pink nose, sharp teeth, a purple tongue, and variously colored spiral-shaped patches all over his body.

characters.name: Bowling Paul
characters.description: Bowling PaulCharacter informationSpeciesImaginary FriendGenderMale ♂Personal StatusCreator(s)Unnamed bowling alley managerMediaVoice ActorPhil LaMarr Bowling Paulis an imaginary friend who was created to be a bowling tutor.Macgoes to him for help after being kicked off ofMadame Foster'sbowling team for not being good at bowling. He only appeared inThe Big Lablooski. Bowling Paul is an imaginary friend with a bowling pin for a head who wears sunglasses. He was imagined by the bowling alley manager to be a bowling tutor, but ended up having no actual skill in the sport.
